Land Rover Range Rover P440e Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover P440e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned P440e (AJ20P6) engine typically costs £6,000–£12,000 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£2,500 depending on workshop location. Battery replacement is separate — typically £8,000–£15,000 once the six-year warranty expires.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Range Rover P440e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20P6 combustion engine itself is a robust Ingenium six-cylinder unit. However, the PHEV system introduces additional complexity. Known issues include BECM failure, electric heater failure, and battery degradation over time. With proper maintenance and software updates, most owners report satisfactory reliability.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Range Rover P440e?

acceptedAnswer.text: BECM (Battery Energy Control Module) failure (causing EV warnings and charging issues), electric heater failure (no cabin heat in EV mode), high-voltage battery degradation, and poor fuel economy when running on petrol alone (mid-20s MPG).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover P440e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 60% Land Rover scrap threshold: if your Range Rover is worth £60,000 and a recon engine is £9,000 (15%), it's absolutely worth it. Even with a £15,000 battery replacement, total cost (£24,000) is 40% of the vehicle's value — still within the threshold. These are high-value vehicles that justify significant repair spend.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Range Rover P440e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The P440e uses the AJ20P6 (also known as PT306) — a 3.0-litre turbocharged straight-six petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the BECM, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The BECM typically requires replacement rather than repair (£1,000–£3,000). It's a critical component that manages the high-voltage battery — a faulty BECM can leave the vehicle undriveable. Always use a JLR specialist with the correct diagnostic equipment for this work.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover P440e Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the only engine generation this badge ever used — the AJ20P6 PHEV. See the Era Map above for era-specific pricing. Note that battery replacement costs are separate — typically £8,000–£15,000 once the warranty expires.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Range Rover P440e engine typically takes 15–25 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The complex PHEV system and high-voltage battery add significant labour time compared to non-hybrid engines.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover P440e engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: BECM (Battery Energy Control Module) Failure
problems.symptoms: "EV temporarily not available" warning, intermittent charging issues, failure to charge to 100%, severe electrical faults, drive system warnings.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — can affect low-mileage examples.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, the vehicle may become undriveable.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if warnings appear.

problems.recommendation: The BECM is a known failure point on Range Rover PHEV models including the P440e. Symptoms can be intermittent and confusing — charging may start and stop unpredictably. If you experience any electrical issues or EV mode warnings, have the BECM inspected immediately. A faulty BECM can leave the vehicle stranded and requires specialist diagnostic equipment.

problems.title: Electric Heater Failure
problems.symptoms: EV mode only available in warm weather, no cabin heat in EV mode, reduced electric range, climate control malfunction.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The electric heater that provides cabin heating in EV mode is a known failure point on JLR PHEVs. When it fails, the car may refuse to enter EV mode unless the climate control is switched off. This is particularly problematic in winter when cabin heat is essential. Have the system inspected if you notice any climate-related EV mode issues.

problems.title: High-Voltage Battery Degradation
problems.symptoms: Reduced EV range, longer charging times, battery warning lights, reduced power in EV mode.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; battery is a wear item.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if range noticeably drops.

problems.recommendation: The 38.2 kWh battery has a six-year/100,000km warranty. Once this expires, replacement costs are significant. To maximise battery life, avoid frequent rapid charging, keep the battery between 20% and 80% for daily use, and ensure the cooling system is maintained. If you notice a significant drop in EV range, have the battery health checked.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover P440e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential repair — vehicle may be undriveable without it
Essential for winter EV use — repair when symptoms appear
Only viable if the rest of the vehicle is in excellent condition
Multiple component failures may exceed vehicle value

buyingChecks.title: Buying This P440e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with JLR-approved PHEV maintenance
* BECM software updates completed and no electrical warnings
* Battery health report showing >85% state of health
* Remaining battery warranty (six years/100,000km from new)
avoidIf: * "EV temporarily not available" or charging warnings — potential BECM issue
* No cabin heat in EV mode — electric heater failure
* Battery range significantly below expected (70 miles WLTP)
* No service history — PHEV systems are complex and unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The P440e is a complex, high-tech luxury SUV that offers impressive EV range but comes with PHEV-specific vulnerabilities. The BECM and electric heater are known weak points, and battery replacement costs are substantial. A £9,000 reconditioned engine on a Range Rover valued at £60,000 is 15% of the car's value — well worth it. However, always factor in potential battery costs when assessing a used example.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover P440e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover P440e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * EV Mode — Test EV-only operation; any warnings or refusal to engage?
* Charging — Does it charge to 100% consistently? Any intermittent stops?
* Cabin Heat — Does the heater work in EV mode? (electric heater failure)
* Service History — Including PHEV-specific maintenance and software updates
* Battery Health — Request a battery health report (state of health)
* Warning Lights — Any BECM, drive system, or hybrid system warnings?
* Fuel Economy — Running on petrol alone should return reasonable MPG
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures and corrosion
